member.name: Sean Woodcock
member.synopsis: Sean Woodcock is the Labour MP for Banbury , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Broadband: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has considered the report published by TYI on 18 March 2026, titled ‘Small Changes, Big Rewards: Ensuring planning connects with mobile infrastructure ambitions’.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On December 18 2025, my Department, in conjunction with the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, launched a joint Call for Evidence on reforming planning rules to accelerate deployment of digital infrastructure. This Call for Evidence closed...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Aerials: Planning Permission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her department has made of the potential economic impact of an increase in notifications to mobile telecommunications companies under the Notice to Quit regime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Electronic Communications Code allows for site providers to serve Notices to Quit in specific limited circumstances, including if land is to be redeveloped and is no longer suitable to host telecommunications apparatus, or needs to be moved. We recogn...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Aerials: Planning Permission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of reforming the Notice to Quit regime to reduce disruption to mobile data coverage and capacity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Electronic Communications Code allows for site providers to serve Notices to Quit in specific limited circumstances, including if land is to be redeveloped and is no longer suitable to host telecommunications apparatus, or needs to be moved. We recogn...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Planning: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many members of the Office of the Parliamentary Counsel have been allocated to support the drafting of (a) primary and (b) secondary legislation in pursuit of planning reform
writtenQuestions.answerText: Within the Office of Parliamentary Counsel (OPC) and Ministry for Housing Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) legal teams there are lawyers working on MHCLG related business, alongside members in the Government Legal Department. There is no group of ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Ukraine: Military Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to increase military support to Ukraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This year the UK will give more military support to Ukraine than ever before, with a £4.5 billion boost. At the 27th Ukraine Defence Contact Group meeting, we announced a rapid surge in support, valued at around £450 million. This follows the £2.26 billio...

writtenQuestions.heading: Chiltern Railways: Overcrowding
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking with Chiltern Railways to help prevent overcrowding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Chiltern Railways periodically adjust their timetable in response to passenger demand. From the 18th May, Chiltern will introduce three new Saturday services between Banbury and London Marylebone. We are engaging closely with Chiltern's fleet renewal prog...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government: Banbury
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps he is taking to ensure the accessibility of Government digital services in Banbury.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Digital Service (GDS) role is to ensure the delivery of digital services that work for all of our citizens. That includes monitoring the services each departments delivers online - to make sure they are providing support for citizens with l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Homelessness: Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department is taking to support veterans affected by homelessness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have increased funding for homelessness services and supported veterans to access social housing by exempting them from local connection tests for social housing in England. The Deputy Prime Minister is chairing an Interministerial Group, which include...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

items.text: Name of donor: Coalition for Global Prosperity (CGP) Address of donor: 1 Horse Guards Avenue, London SW1A 2HU Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£883), accommodation (£649.90), food and refreshments (£270.23), value £1,803.13 Destination of visit: Jordan Dates of visit: 20 July 2025 to 25 July 2025 Purpose of visit: Overseas visit to Jordan to see UK Development programmes ‘in-action’ and meet with development experts and partners. (Registered 14 August 2025)

Name of donor: Armed Forces Parliamentary Trust (AFPT) Address of donor: Houses of Parliament, London SW1A 0AA Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Return flights between London Heathrow and Badar Seri Bagawan, value £864.39 Destination of visit: Brunei Dates of visit: 24 May 2026 to 29 May 2026 Purpose of visit: I undertook this visit as part of my participation in the Armed Forces Parliamentary Scheme. Training and learning to understand more about the work of the UK Armed Forces in Brunei. (Registered 4 June 2026)
